title: acer dasycarpum subvar. normale
intro: Acer dasycarpum subvar. normale, also known as the Chinese paperbark maple, is a variety of deciduous maple tree native to China. It is valued for its bark, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Acer dasycarpum subvar. normale include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the bark, such as infusions and decoctions, are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
